name: Segura de la Sierra from Siles - circular via the Morles river
name_original: Segura de la Sierra desde Siles — circular por el río de Morles
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Siles are situated in a region characterized by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys, forming part of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The landscape features significant elevation changes, offering challenging climbs and descents for mountain bikers. This area, located in Jaén, Spain, provides a diverse environment for outdoor activities. The routes often traverse natural parks, showcasing the varied topography of the Siles region. Best mountain bike trails around Siles The most popular mountain bike trail is The Cerro de los Calarejos from Siles - circular route through the Natural Park of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura and las Villas, a difficult 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 34 minutes to complete. This circular route offers extensive views within the Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Segura de la Sierra from Siles - circular via the Morles river, a difficult 30.9 miles (49.7 km) path. This route follows the Morles river, providing a scenic journey through the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Dehesa del Oso and Alto de Cambrón Loop from Siles, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Siles is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Siles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Siles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Siles?
A: There are 18 mountain bike trails around Siles, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, with one trail specifically categorized as easy.

Q: What are the general characteristics of mountain biking terrain in Siles?
A: Mountain biking in Siles is defined by its mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys, all within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. Riders can expect significant elevation changes, providing both challenging climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Siles?
A: Yes, there is one easy mountain bike trail available around Siles. While most routes are moderate to difficult, this option allows beginners to enjoy the region's natural beauty at a more relaxed pace.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Siles?
A: The trails around Siles offer access to stunning natural monuments. You might encounter impressive waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltaor Waterfall (Zurriero)&lt;/a&gt;, explore unique geological features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5974287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorros Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region around Siles is rich in history, featuring several castles. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5239771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6536732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Keep Tower of Hornos de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from or near mountain biking areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Siles?
A: The mountain bike trails in Siles are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 12 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the immersive experience within the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Siles?
A: Yes, many routes around Siles are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211840924/the-cerro-de-los-calarejos-from-siles-circular-route-through-the-natural-park-of-the-sierra-de-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de los Calarejos from Siles&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211875475/segura-de-la-sierra-from-siles-circular-via-the-morles-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de la Sierra from Siles&lt;/a&gt; route follows the scenic Morles river.

Q: What is a good challenging mountain bike route for experienced riders in Siles?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211915642/dehesa-del-oso-and-alto-de-cambron-loop-from-siles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa del Oso and Alto de Cambrón Loop from Siles&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 23.3-mile (37.5 km) trail features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by a tower or historical structure?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siles – El Cubo Tower, Siles loop from Siles&lt;/a&gt; is a route that takes you past the historic El Cubo Tower, offering a blend of natural scenery and cultural interest.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails around Siles?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails around Siles varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, a challenging 31.2-mile (50.2 km) route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211840924/the-cerro-de-los-calarejos-from-siles-circular-route-through-the-natural-park-of-the-sierra-de-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cerro de los Calarejos&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours and 34 minutes to complete, while shorter routes will be quicker.

Q: Is there a mountain bike route that includes a summit?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17266255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m) – Era del Fustal Shelter loop from PR-A 176 Acebeas - Cumbre de Navalperal&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 30.7-mile (49.4 km) mountain bike trail that takes you to the El Yelmo Summit, offering spectacular views.
tips: The Peña del Olivar forest and recreation park on the outskirts of Siles is a great place to reconnect with nature. Take a bath in the natural pool on a warm and sunny day.

Segura de la Sierra is a Spanish town and municipality in the province of Jaén, in the autonomous community of Andalusia, belonging to the region of Sierra de Segura, located northeast of Andalusia. According to INE sources, in 2017 it had 1,807 inhabitants. Its municipal area, with an area of 225.02 km², is the most disintegrated in the province, having four exclaves apart from the main nucleus. The five enclaves are within the Natural Park of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas and encompass a total of 18 population centers, 13 of them populated, with the Cortijos Nuevos district being the largest.
This section is a path that navigates the side of the mountain with views of Orcera.
Siles is an Andalusian border town on the edge of the Los Calares del Mundo and La Sima natural park. Perfect place to start your adventure.
